ai_enrichment.summary: A scheduled task was created to execute a payload from a Temp directory with SYSTEM privileges, triggered at user logon. The task was created via PowerShell executing an Atomic Red Team test script, indicating either adversary simulation testing or actual malicious persistence establishment. The combination of SYSTEM-level execution and Temp directory payload is highly suspicious.
investigation_steps: Verify if this is an authorized Atomic Red Team test by checking with the red team or security testing schedule
Examine payload.exe in the Temp directory: collect hash, check file metadata, submit to sandbox/VT
Query scheduled tasks on endpoint: schtasks /query /tn T1053_005_OnLogon /v /fo LIST
Review parent PowerShell script content and determine how it was initiated
Check for other persistence mechanisms created in the same timeframe
Analyze process tree to identify initial access vector if not authorized testing
containment_actions: Delete the scheduled task immediately: schtasks /delete /tn T1053_005_OnLogon /f
If unauthorized, isolate the endpoint from the network
Block the payload hash at the endpoint protection level
Disable the labuser account pending investigation if compromise is suspected
ai_enrichment.false_positive_reasoning: The AtomicRedTeam directory path and T1053.005 naming convention strongly suggest this is a controlled adversary simulation test rather than actual malicious activity.
